business_description.nature_of_business: Commercial construction contractor with operations in carpentry, electrical wiring, plumbing, masonry, interior painting, and project management/superintendence

coverages_discussed.notes: Policy Number WC-KCB-2025-5580; Large Risk rating plan under PCRB; Final Experience Modification Factor 0.86

positives: Experience modification factor of 0.86 indicates better-than-average loss experience
Significant clerical office payroll exposure (low-hazard class 8810)
Substantial project management/superintendent payroll suggesting active supervision
concerns: Multiple higher-hazard construction class codes including carpentry, masonry, and electrical
Claim frequency of 10 claims in policy year 2021-2022
Total incurred losses exceeding $548,000 across the three-year experience period
risk_highlights.loss_history_summary: Three-year loss experience: 2023-2024: 7 claims, $191,231 incurred; 2022-2023: 3 claims, $116,479 incurred; 2021-2022: 10 claims, $240,694 incurred. Total actual primary losses $335,671 and actual excess losses $212,733.

extracted_text_sections.executive_summary: PCRB Experience Rating Worksheet for Keystone Commercial Builders, Inc. under policy WC-KCB-2025-5580 (policy period 2025-08-01 to 2026-08-01). Valuation date 2026-03-15. Calculated experience modification factor of 0.8600, rounded to final Experience Modification Factor of 0.86.
extracted_text_sections.operations_description: Pennsylvania-based commercial builder with workers compensation payroll distributed across: Carpentry NOC (5403) $5,915,000; Electrical Wiring (5190) $4,544,000; Plumbing NOC (5183) $3,075,000; Masonry NOC (5022) $6,635,850; Interior Painting (5474) $1,639,000; Project Manager/Superintendent (5606) $4,776,000; and Clerical Office (8810) $5,010,000. Total payroll $31,594,850.
extracted_text_sections.claims_narrative: Actual Loss Data by policy year: 2023-2024 had 7 claims with $190,770 paid and $191,231 incurred ($120,455 primary / $70,776 excess); 2022-2023 had 3 claims with $115,267 paid and $116,479 incurred ($58,500 primary / $57,979 excess); 2021-2022 had 10 claims with $240,694 paid and incurred ($156,716 primary / $83,978 excess). Rating calculation uses Expected Losses of $1,095,520, Weighting Factor 0.574, Ballast Value $13,748, Expected Excess Losses $1,132,496, yielding numerator $953,971 and denominator $1,109,268.
